eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a narrow 33.6% implied probability for April 2026 CPI annual inflation at 3.7%, closely trailed by 3.8% (25.9%) and 3.6% (21.0%), reflecting tight competition amid sticky price pressures. March 2026 CPI surged to 3.3% year-over-year—up sharply from February's 2.4%—driven by a 21% energy goods spike from geopolitical tensions elevating oil prices near $108 per barrel. Core CPI held at 2.6% year-over-year, with persistent shelter costs offsetting modest goods disinflation. Recent consumer sentiment plunge and elevated five-year inflation expectations at 3.5% have sustained upward bias, while Federal Reserve officials highlight 3% sticky inflation risks ahead of the May 12 release. Key swing factors include April energy trajectories and services pricing.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NM-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NM-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

Stansbury, who won re-election in 2024 by 56.4% and previous races by similar double-digit margins, faces no Democratic primary challengers on June 2, while Republican Ndidiamaka Okpareke stands alone after rivals withdrew, hampered by a stark fundraising gap—Stansbury's $356,000 cash on hand dwarfs Okpareke's $30,000 as of late March. Recent pre-primary conventions solidified these dynamics, with minimal shifts since. Upsets remain possible via Stansbury scandal, health issues, or a powerful national Republican wave favoring midterms incumbency vulnerability.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 65-89 tweets from April 30 to May 2, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 30 12:00 PM ET to May 2, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will SpaceX raise less than $40B in its IPO?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total gross proceeds raised by SpaceX in its initial public offering (IPO) at the time of pricing.

The amount raised is defined as the aggregate dollar value of shares sold at the final offering price at the time of IPO pricing, as disclosed in the final prospectus filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Shares sold pursuant to any overallotment option (greenshoe) will not be considered.

Secondary sales of existing shares by current shareholders will be included only to the extent that they are part of the IPO offering at pricing and reflected in the total gross proceeds. Private secondary transactions conducted outside of the IPO will not be considered.

If the total proceeds fall exactly on a boundary between two ranges, this market will resolve to the higher range.

If SpaceX does not complete an IPO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.

The primary resolution source will be the final prospectus filed with the SEC; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

The closely contested odds stem from SpaceX's April 1 confidential S-1 filing and accelerated employee share vesting—signaling a June roadshow and potential mid-month pricing—yet tempered by sky-high IPO valuation targets of 1.75-2 trillion dollars that imply revenue multiples exceeding 100 times. Key swing factors include the balance of primary versus secondary shares, Starlink revenue trajectory, and broader equity market appetite ahead of the anticipated public filing this May.

markets.question: Nord Stream pipeline turned on before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any line of either Nord Stream 1 or Nord Stream 2 delivers natural gas to Germany or any EU member state in commercial quantities at any point by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

"Commercial quantities" refers to sustained, measurable flows of gas intended for end-user distribution. Test flows, pressure tests, or symbolic gas transfers not tied to actual supply operations will not count.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Nord Stream pipeline turned on before 2027?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a 92.5% chance against the Nord Stream pipelines resuming operations before 2027, driven by unrepaired sabotage damage from 2022 explosions, ongoing German investigations into the incident, and Berlin's repeated official rejections of revival amid the Ukraine war. Nord Stream 2 AG remains in legal insolvency, with a Swiss court delaying liquidation without resolution, while U.S. sanctions persist and EU diversification to U.S. LNG and Norwegian gas has reduced reliance on Russian supplies. Sporadic 2025 Russia-U.S. talks and Kremlin claims of quick activation for an intact pipe segment failed to materialize into action, reinforcing trader skepticism absent a Ukraine ceasefire or major diplomatic breakthrough.

markets.question: Will xAI have the best AI model at the end of June 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company which owns the model which has the highest arena rank based off the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on June 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" section on the Leaderboard tab of https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with the style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

This market will resolve to "CD Guadalajara" if CD Guadalajara win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Tigres de la UANL".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligamx.net. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

Tigres enjoy full roster availability with no international absences, enabling all nine foreigners, while Chivas' strong form and historical Liguilla edge over Tigres add upset potential in the two-legged aggregate series.

markets.question: Will Russia capture all of Pokrovsk by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the entirety of Pokrovsk, Donetsk Oblast, (48.303080° N, 37.192568° E) by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET.

Pokrovsk will be considered captured if the entirety of the municipality is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by the resolution date, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. “Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine” will not qualify.

The territory of the municipality is shaded in light grey and is distinguished from bordering municipalities with a darker grey line. If all area within the municipality is shaded in red, however, the shading does not precisely match up with the border such that there is a tiny amount of grey along the border of the city, this will still qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

Once Russia captures the entirety of the specified area, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Location on Google maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/rwDQehLsZAUsquVX7

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

President Zelenskyy revealed on April 9 that Moscow tasked troops with capturing Pokrovsk, Kostyantynivka, and Druzhkivka by month-end but lacks sufficient forces, drawing from reserves amid stalled momentum. The frontline remains contested roughly 4-6 km east of the city, with trader consensus reflecting Ukraine's defensive edge despite attritional pressure and the looming deadline.

markets.question: Will Iran strike Dimona by April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Iran carries out a kinetic military strike on the listed facility between market creation and April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This includes, but is not limited to, drone and missile strikes, aerial bombings. Cyber attacks, sanctions, or diplomatic actions will not count towards the resolution of this market.

For the purposes of this market, only strikes that cause physical damage to the infrastructure of the facility will be considered. Missile and drone strikes that are shot down or miss their targets, or make contact in open spaces or around the facility, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory, will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks by proxy forces (i.e., Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Note: Cyber attacks will not qualify, even if they have kinetic effects.

Tehran has launched no verified kinetic strikes since, focusing threats on retaliation for blockade enforcement and potential new strikes, with Iranian commanders vowing April-dated missiles if hostilities resume. Recent escalatory signals include April 28 Iran-linked cyberattacks on US troops and anti-aircraft fire over Tehran amid US Central Command strike plans. Traders assess slim odds of Iranian action by April 30 without provocation, as negotiations falter and ceasefire deadlines loom.

markets.question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in another EU country?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no in-person meeting between President Trump and President Putin by June 30, driven by stalled Ukraine peace talks and Trump's focus on the U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran, with no bilateral summit announced despite recent diplomacy. Their latest face-to-face was the August 2025 Alaska summit, which yielded no deal, followed by a cancelled Budapest plan; recent developments include Putin's envoy visiting Washington in early April for negotiations and a April 29 phone call discussing a potential Ukraine "Victory Day" truce and Iran issues, but only via phone or proxies. A U.S. invitation for Putin to the December G20 in Miami falls after resolution. Barriers include mutual distrust, war escalations, and logistical challenges for neutral sites, though a sudden Ukraine breakthrough or neutral-host offer like Turkey could shift odds.

events.title: Argentina vs. Algeria
events.description: This event is for the upcoming FIFA World Cup game, scheduled for Tuesday, June 16, 2026 between Argentina and Algeria.

eventMetadata.context_description: Argentina's status as reigning World Cup champions and superior squad depth, anchored by Lionel Messi and a deep midfield, drive trader consensus to a 68.5% implied probability of victory in their Group J opener against Algeria on June 16 at Arrowhead Stadium. Recent injury woes have tempered enthusiasm slightly, with Cristian Romero sidelined for the season via MCL tear, Joaquin Panichelli out with ACL damage, and concerns over Lisandro Martinez and Emiliano Martinez, though Lautaro Martinez has returned from muscular issues. Algeria faces a steeper uphill battle at 10%, exacerbated by a goalkeeping crisis—Anthony Mandrea post-shoulder surgery, Melvin Mastil with groin hernia, and Luca Zidane doubtful after jaw fracture—plus absences like Amine Gouiri and Yacine Titraoui, leaving the draw at 19% as a competitive hedge in this neutral-site clash. Historical edge favors Argentina, unbeaten in their lone prior meeting.

markets.question: Will UK GDP growth in Q1 2026 be between 0% and 0.3%?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the UK real gross domestic product (GDP) growth rate (% change) for Q1 of 2026 compared with the same quarter a year ago in the "GDP First quarterly estimate, UK" release for Q1 of 2026, scheduled for May 14, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/grossdomesticproductgdp/bulletins/gdpfirstquarterlyestimateuk/previousreleases

If no data for the specified quarter is released by the date the next quarter's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available quarter.

Note: data from the initial release of the referenced GDP report is what will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following quarter or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the initial release will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders' skin-in-the-game consensus prices UK quarter-on-quarter GDP growth for Q1 2026 (Jan-Mar) in a razor-thin contest, with 1.2-1.5% at 47.2% implied probability edging 0.6-0.9% at 44.6%, reflecting February's robust 0.5% monthly expansion—beating forecasts and revised January up to 0.1%—which cumulatively suggests 0.6%+ potential absent a weak March. However, March composite PMI eased to 51.0 from February's 53.7, signaling growth slowdown amid Middle East conflict-fueled energy inflation spikes, tempering upside while keeping lower bins viable. Bank of England holds Bank Rate at 3.75% tomorrow amid persistent price pressures; ONS preliminary Q1 release on May 14 remains the pivotal catalyst to resolve positioning.

markets.question: Will Doja Cat have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the PA-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the PA-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Democrat Chrissy Houlahan's strong reelection bid in Pennsylvania's 6th Congressional District drives the 92.5% trader consensus favoring the Democratic Party, reflecting her consistent 56% general election margins since 2018, superior fundraising with $3.9 million cash on hand versus Republican Marty Young's $176,000, and the district's D+6 partisan lean encompassing Democratic-trending Chester County suburbs. Rated Solid Democratic by Cook Political Report and others, PA-06 lacks competitive polling or recent catalysts, with Houlahan facing no viable Democratic primary opponent ahead of the May 19 contest and Young as the sole GOP filer showing limited traction. Scenarios to shift odds include a Houlahan scandal, health issue, or extraordinary national Republican midterm wave overcoming her biographical appeal as an Air Force veteran and moderate.

markets.question: Will Microsoft (MSFT) close above $435 end of April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) on the final trading day of April 2026 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the final trading day of the month is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/MSFT/history, published under "Historical Prices."

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

markets.question: Will UBS or any of its underwriting affiliates serve as the lead underwriter in SpaceX’s initial public offering?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the bank that serves as the lead underwriter in the initial public offering of SpaceX.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or SpaceX completes an initial public offering without a designated lead underwriter, this market will resolve to “Other.”

If multiple banks are identified as lead underwriters, this market will resolve according to the primary lead underwriter. If the hierarchy between them is unclear, this market will resolve once it is conclusively evident which bank is the primary lead underwriter, for example, through the order in which the banks are listed in the underwriting section of SpaceX’s final initial public offering prospectus, once released.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official disclosures from SpaceX. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket positions Morgan Stanley at 47% implied probability as the lead bank for SpaceX's anticipated IPO, driven by its prominent role as an active bookrunner in the "Project Apex" syndicate alongside Goldman Sachs, Bank of America, JPMorgan, and Citigroup, as reported in early April 2026. Morgan Stanley's edge stems from leading three recent SpaceX tender offers—more than Bank of America’s two—and banker Michael Grimes' track record with Elon Musk ventures like Tesla. Bank of America follows at 22.6% due to its senior underwriting position, while Goldman Sachs at 13% reflects jockeying for control highlighted in late April reports. No final lead is confirmed ahead of a potential June 2026 listing, leaving room for shifts based on deal structuring and retail access via E*Trade.

events.title: Le Mans FC vs. Stade de Reims - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Ligue 2 game, scheduled for May 2 at 2:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Le Mans FC, entrenched in third place on the Ligue 2 table with 15 wins and robust home form (eight victories at Stade Marie-Marvingt), host fourth-placed Stade de Reims on May 2 in a pivotal promotion six-pointer. Le Mans boast momentum from recent triumphs including a 4-0 rout of Pau FC on April 6 and a 4-3 comeback at Amiens, bolstering their clean-sheet streak. Reims remain competitive but stumbled to a 2-2 draw at Laval last week, compounded by off-field disruption from armed fan threats at their training ground five days ago potentially impacting morale. Reims dominate head-to-head (unbeaten in six, including February's 3-0 Coupe de France win), though Le Mans contend without long-term absentee Jean Vercruysse (cruciate ligament tear).

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Hong Kong be 21°C on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 30 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Max (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Observatory's latest forecast update early on April 30 projects a maximum temperature around 26°C under mainly cloudy skies and moderate to fresh northeasterly winds from an incoming monsoon, tempering highs after April 29's observed peak of 28.4°C amid a recent warm spell. This positions the market-implied 75% probability for 25°C or higher as trader consensus on the official model's baseline, with 19.5% odds on exactly 24°C reflecting minor downside risks from cloud cover or offshore gusts reducing solar heating. Late-April climatology supports above-normal temperatures, but the northeast flow introduces short-term cooling; real-time observations from the Observatory will refine this as the day progresses toward evening resolution.

markets.question: Will Valtteri Bottas be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' George Russell and Kimi Antonelli dominate trader consensus for the 2026 F1 Drivers' Championship, holding 41.5% and 31.1% implied probabilities after leading the driver standings through the first three Grands Prix—Aussie, Chinese, and Japanese. Antonelli tops the table with 72 points from back-to-back victories in China (ignoring team orders) and Japan, plus every pole position, showcasing blistering one-lap pace in the superior Mercedes under new regulations, while Russell sits second on 63 points with an Australian win and Sprint triumph, bolstered by stronger race starts and consistency. Leclerc trails at 49 points in third for Ferrari, but Mercedes' clear pace edge leaves challengers like Verstappen, Piastri, and Norris with slim 3-6% chances amid early constructors' dominance. Recent intra-team harmony and no major reliability issues sustain the tight duo battle heading to Miami.

markets.question: Will Ted Cruz post 200+ posts from April 28 to May 5, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Ted Cruz (@tedcruz), posts on X between April 28, 12:00 PM ET and May 5, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus clusters tightly around 60-119 posts for Ted Cruz (@tedcruz) on X from April 28 12:00 PM ET through May 5 12:00 PM ET, reflecting his consistent 10-15 daily average amid routine Senate duties, podcast promotions like Verdict, and reactive commentary on breaking news. Recent drivers include April 28-29 posts surging on King Charles III's joint session address to Congress, FBI raids on 22 Somali-linked fraud sites in Minneapolis tied to Rep. Ilhan Omar and Gov. Tim Walz, plus Schumer's ICE remarks—pushing early volume higher and mirroring prior weeks' 80-99 and apparent 100-119 tallies. The race stays neck-and-neck due to variable news cycles; separation could stem from intensified committee hearings or floor votes this week versus quieter periods or travel.

markets.question: UFC Fight Night: Dom Mar Fan vs. Kody Steele (Lightweight, Prelims)
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Dom Mar Fan" if Dom Mar Fan is officially declared the winner of the fight against Kody Steele at UFC Fight Night: Della Maddalena vs. Prates, scheduled for May 2, 2026.

It will resolve to "Kody Steele" if Kody Steele is officially declared the winner.

If the fight is declared a draw or technical draw, ruled a No Contest, not scored, canceled, or postponed beyond May 16, 2026, this market will resolve "50-50."

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Dom Mar Fan makes his UFC debut on home soil at UFC Perth's lightweight prelims against Kody Steele, riding a five-fight win streak capped by his Road to UFC Season 4 lightweight tournament victory via unanimous decision over Sang Uk Kim in January. The 26-year-old Australian boasts a 5'11" frame, 75-inch reach, and BJJ black belt credentials, positioning him to exploit Steele's 15-month layoff since a competitive unanimous decision loss to Rong Zhu in UFC 312's Fight of the Night. Steele, a 7-1 Contender Series graduate and fellow jiu-jitsu black belt, seeks his first Octagon win amid travel fatigue to RAC Arena, where trader consensus reflects stylistic grappling clash and Fan's momentum versus Steele's experience. Recent fight-week interviews underscore Fan's prediction of a bloody finish, with no reported injuries ahead of weigh-ins.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the SC-02 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the SC-02 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors the Republican Party at 82% to win South Carolina's 2nd Congressional District House seat, reflecting the district's R+7 partisan voting index, Donald Trump's 56%-42% margin in 2024, and Solid Republican ratings from Cook Political Report, Inside Elections, and Sabato's Crystal Ball. Incumbent Joe Wilson dominates the June 9 Republican primary field against challengers Sam Gibbons and Hamp Redmond, boasting $669,000 in fundraising through March 31—far outpacing rivals—fueling uncertainty among specific candidates labeled A, B, and Other near 50%. Democrats trail at 17.5% amid a crowded primary of Zyon Khalifa, Roger Pruitt, David Robinson II, and Daniel Shrief with negligible cash on hand post-March 30 filing deadline, alongside Workers Party's Dayna Alane Smith. Primaries on June 9 and general election November 3 loom as key catalysts.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve held its federal funds rate steady at 3.50%–3.75% following the April 29, 2026 FOMC meeting, reflecting trader consensus amid sticky inflation evidenced by March CPI rising 3.3% year-over-year—above expectations—and a resilient labor market with unemployment at 4.3%. Polymarket traders, wagering over $1 million, price in a gradual easing path aligned with the March dot plot projecting 3.00%–3.25% by end-2027, driven by cooling but persistent price pressures and solid nonfarm payrolls. Key risks include upcoming May 12 CPI data and the June 16–17 FOMC, where hotter readings could delay cuts, while softening jobless claims might accelerate them, underscoring markets' probabilistic pricing of monetary policy divergence from peak 2025 levels.

markets.question: Will New England Patriots win the 2026 AFC East?

This market will resolve according to the team that is declared as the AFC East division champion for the 2026 NFL regular season.

If multiple teams tie atop the division, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the NFL.

If the 2026 NFL regular season is cancelled, postponed after January 17, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this division within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NFL; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Buffalo Bills hold a slim 53.5% implied probability as AFC East favorites, buoyed by Josh Allen's elite quarterback play and key offseason additions like trading for WR D.J. Moore and signing DE Bradley Chubb, despite a middling D+ draft grade with edge rusher T.J. Parker and CB Davison Igbinosun. New England Patriots surged to 36% after snapping Buffalo's streak with the 2025 division title, bolstered by promising draft picks and free agency depth amid rumors of WR A.J. Brown landing there, narrowing the gap in this closely contested market. Jets (8%) and Dolphins (6.6%) lag amid QB turmoil—New York's $48M dead cap from releasing Aaron Rodgers and Justin Fields, Miami's $52M hit post-Tua Tagovailoa trade to Atlanta.

markets.question: Series: Anyone's Legend Kill Handicap (-10.5) vs Weibo Gaming (+10.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Anyone's Legend and Weibo Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, scheduled for January 20 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend secures 11 or more kills than Weibo Gaming in the match. 

Otherwise, it will resolve to "Weibo Gaming".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

events.title: LoL: Anyone's Legend vs Weibo Gaming (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend win against Weibo Gaming in the named LoL match in the LPL Group Ascend, scheduled for January 20 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Weibo Gaming" if Weibo Gaming win against Anyone's Legend in the LoL match.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Main_Page.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anyone's Legend holds the edge in trader consensus for their LPL Split 2 Group Ascend BO3 against Weibo Gaming, riding a hot streak with 2-0 sweeps over Invictus Gaming and Ninjas in Pyjamas in the past week, propelling them into top-three standings at 5-3 series record. Weibo Gaming secured a morale-boosting 2-0 over Top Esports but sits precariously at 3-6 amid roster upheaval, losing jungler Tian and support Erha in late March. AL boasts a 57% head-to-head win rate, including a 2-0 earlier this split, with Tarzan's jungle pathing and macro sidelane pressure key factors. Top-four spots offer direct playoff berths, heightening stakes under Patch 26.7 meta shifts.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Manila be 32°C on May 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Ninoy Aquino International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 1 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Ninoy Aquino International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/ph/manila/RPLL.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: PAGASA's latest forecast projects a maximum air temperature of 35°C at Port Area, Manila on May 1, 2026, under persistent easterlies fostering partly cloudy skies and just a 10% rain chance, driving trader consensus toward 36°C (54.5% implied probability) and 35°C (25.5%) as leading outcomes. Recent daily highs near 35°C, including April 29's projected peak, combined with May's climatological norm of 33-34°C averages but frequent excursions to 36°C or higher due to high solar insolation and urban heat effects, explain the slight overweight on 36°C despite model projections. Historical records show May peaks up to 38.5°C, underscoring uncertainty; watch PAGASA's daily 5 a.m. updates for refined guidance ahead of resolution based on official station data.

markets.question: Les Wexner charged by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or otherwise announces a criminal indictment of Leslie Wexner between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purposes of this market the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources, however a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors no criminal charges against Les Wexner by June 30, reflecting the absence of any active DOJ investigation or indictment despite heightened scrutiny from 2026 Epstein files releases. In February, the House Oversight Committee deposed Wexner, where he denied knowledge of Epstein's crimes and claimed to have been duped, with documents revealing past FBI labeling as an unindicted co-conspirator but limited evidence and no prior prosecution. A civil lawsuit filed by Epstein victims in early April alleges Wexner enabled trafficking, yet distinguishes from federal criminal proceedings, underscoring procedural barriers and historical non-pursuit amid congressional probes rather than DOJ action.

markets.question: Will Africa win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the continent of the country that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup, currently scheduled for June 11-July 19, 2026.

For example, if France wins the tournament, the market will resolve to Europe.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The definitive source of the continent for each country will be World Population Review (https://worldpopulationreview.com/country-rankings/list-of-countries-by-continent).

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/) and the World Population Review; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Europe at 72.5% implied probability to claim the 2026 FIFA World Cup trophy, driven by UEFA's unmatched depth with all 16 qualifiers secured—including powerhouses Spain, France, England, Germany, Portugal, and recent play-off winners Bosnia and Herzegovina, Czechia, Sweden, and Türkiye—bolstering a field historically responsible for 12 titles. South America's 20.5% reflects CONMEBOL's six elite entrants like defending champions Argentina, Brazil, Uruguay, and Colombia, offering quality despite fewer slots. Lower odds for Asia (9 teams including Japan, South Korea), Africa (10 led by Morocco, Senegal), North America (hosts USA, Canada, Mexico plus Panama), and Oceania (New Zealand) stem from qualification concluded March 31 via tense inter-confederation play-offs, where no outsider disrupted the established hierarchy of talent and form heading into the expanded 48-team group stage.

markets.question: Counter-Strike: SINQU vs The Last Resort (BO1) - ESEA Advanced Europe Regular Season
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between SINQU and The Last Resort in the ESEA Advanced Europe Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 29 at 2:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "SINQU" if SINQU win the match against The Last Resort.

This market will resolve to "The Last Resort" if The Last Resort win the match against SINQU.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

The single-map format heightens upset potential in this mid-table positioning battle.

markets.question: Mauthausen: Martin Krumich vs Jaime Faria
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Martin Krumich and Jaime Faria in the Mauthausen, originally scheduled for April 29, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Martin Krumich' if Martin Krumich advances against Jaime Faria.

This market will resolve to 'Jaime Faria' if Jaime Faria advances against Martin Krumich.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market refers to the tennis match between Martin Krumich and Jaime Faria in the Mauthausen, originally scheduled for April 29, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Martin Krumich' if Martin Krumich advances against Jaime Faria.

This market will resolve to 'Jaime Faria' if Jaime Faria advances against Martin Krumich.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jaime Faria's superior ATP ranking of 136 compared to Martin Krumich's 320 anchors trader consensus at 63.5% implied probability for their Mauthausen Challenger round of 16 clay-court clash. Faria advanced in the round of 32 by defeating qualifier Andrea Guerrieri in three sets, leveraging his experience from recent deep Challenger runs including finals appearances on clay. Krumich, on a hot streak, powered through qualifying with straight-set wins over Ryan Nijboer and Trey Hilderbrand before a gritty 7-5, 6-7(7), 6-2 victory over Marko Topo, fueling upset potential. No head-to-head history or injury concerns shape a competitive matchup where Faria's overall level edges sentiment despite Krumich's momentum.
